Pariprashnena — Q&A Archive

A read-only archive of 1,235 questions and 14,977 answers from a Gauḍīya Vaiṣṇava forum (2007–2012).

is there a unified standard for Deity worship?

Sadhana · asked by user [] · 2007-08-19 · 14 answers
or there are many and all valid...
user [38] · 2007-08-20
ISKCON Deity Worship Ministry: http://deityworship.com/
user [2] · 2007-08-20
very nice site! the contact page gives a 404 though
user [19] · 2007-08-20
I think Nrsimha Kavaca P. is in charge of the site and the deity worship ministry, its standards etc.
user [40] · 2007-08-20
There is always Pancaratra Pradipa in 2 vols

Method of Deity worship for the International Society of Krishna Consciousness

compiled by the GBC Deity Worship Research Group.

Hard to get, I think it is Vedabase. Maybe someone can confirm.
user [38] · 2007-08-20
Yes. Mail: NKD at pamho.net
user [33] · 2007-08-20
[quote][cite] abrennan:[/cite]There is always Pancaratra Pradipa in 2 vols

Method of Deity worship for the International Society of Krishna Consciousness

compiled by the GBC Deity Worship Research Group.

Hard to get, I think it is Vedabase. Maybe someone can confirm.[/quote]

Yes, the set is on the Vedabase. I was able to locate the set some years back in a used book store in new condition!
user [106] · 2007-08-20
I guess that bhakti will do in any circumstance, isnt?
Of course one may not have it, but still we can try.... A nice quote from Sacinandana Swami (at the latest Govardhana retreat) relating a pastime of the Vrajabasis... Someone asked a gopi regarding worshiping Krishna, the gopi replied we dont worship Him, we love Him...
user [2] · 2007-08-20
what about, "If one offers Me with love and devotion a leaf, a flower, fruit or water, I will accept it. "
That sounds to me as the unified standard of worship.
user [40] · 2007-08-21
[quote][cite] Hari Bhakta dasa:[/cite][quote][cite] abrennan:[/cite]There is always Pancaratra Pradipa in 2 vols

Method of Deity worship for the International Society of Krishna Consciousness[/quote]

Yes, the set is on the Vedabase. I was able to locate the set some years back in a used book store in new condition![/quote]

In used book store! I had to search and beg then call a few friendly BBT folk who located a set in India and got a devotee to bring them when he came back (I think)

Im saving up for Vedabase ;)
user [38] · 2007-08-21
The DW site says that PP is now under revision due this Kartik.
user [40] · 2007-08-21
Whats the DW site, please
user [38] · 2007-08-21
deityworship.com (above)
user [140] · 2007-08-21
..yes, Iskcon has, or use to have an official publication on the matter.
user [33] · 2007-08-22
[quote][cite] abrennan:[/cite][quote][cite] Hari Bhakta dasa:[/cite][quote][cite] abrennan:[/cite]There is always Pancaratra Pradipa in 2 vols

Method of Deity worship for the International Society of Krishna Consciousness[/quote]

Yes, the set is on the Vedabase. I was able to locate the set some years back in a used book store in new condition![/quote]

In used book store! I had to search and beg then call a few friendly BBT folk who located a set in India and got a devotee to bring them when he came back (I think)

Im saving up for Vedabase ;)[/quote]

Yup, used book store! I audibly gasped when I found the books there in apparently unused condition. Thrift stores and used books stores can be surprising sources of transcendental treasures.
